spec_match.rationale: The page accurately frames the current Chrome 150 contract as listbox focusgroup syntax plus a JS row/column helper, not a native 2D grid focusgroup behavior.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified one-Tab-stop entry, Left/Right/Up/Down movement, Enter/Space selection, aria-selected updates, and focusgroupstart updates in both pickers.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature and concept copy state that native 2D grid behavior is outside the current Chrome 150 focusgroup contract.

edge_cases.rationale: The page covers current listbox-focusgroup behavior, older-browser row/column fallback, color and emoji picker use cases, and explicitly avoids claiming a future native grid contract.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the current scoped Open UI explainer.

design_system.rationale: Picker surfaces keep the shared CSS system, clear focus outlines, and visual color swatches as demo content.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verification used Chrome Canary 151 with experimental web platform features enabled. Color and emoji pickers each behaved as one Tab stop, arrow movement and selection worked, aria-selected/focusgroupstart updated, and console/network checks were clean.
